description_long: This object is a weathered stone grave marker, likely a fragment of a larger monument, resting horizontally on a rectangular patch of earth with scattered fallen leaves. The stone is rectangular with a partially broken end and features a carved, linear relief along its side, possibly once part of a decorative border. Moss and lichen growth are visible on the upper surfaces, indicating significant age and exposure to the elements. The base is surrounded by a patch of grass and soil, with the stone itself showing discoloration and erosion typical of outdoor graveyard monuments. The overall appearance suggests it is a remnant of a classical or Victorian-style grave marker, now lying flat rather than upright. No visible inscriptions or symbols are discernible from the provided views.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone grave marker fragment with carved relief, moss, and lichen, resting on a grassy patch with scattered leaves.
description_view_0: The side view highlights the carved relief and moss growth along the length of the stone, with the broken end visible on the right.
description_view_1: The end view shows the narrow width of the stone and the central groove or channel running along its top surface.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays a plainer surface with less visible relief, emphasizing the stone's rectangular shape and weathered texture.
description_view_3: The rear end view reveals the blocky, broken end of the stone and the continuation of the groove, with more moss and lichen visible.
